methods.name: DATAPUMP

eligibility.reason: Logical migration path is available when source and target versions are known

positive_weights.reason: Subset migration favors logical export and import
Remapping favors Data Pump
Reorganization is easier with logical migration
Non-full-database migration favors logical tooling
Smaller databases with generous downtime are strong Data Pump candidates
Version upgrade gaps are easier to absorb with logical export and import
Cross-platform moves commonly favor logical migration

negative_weights.reason: Very large databases increase export and import duration risk
Tight downtime window weakens Data Pump fit
TDE mismatch adds extra wallet and encryption setup effort for logical migration

prerequisites: Network throughput validated for dump transfer

positive_weights.reason: Ultra-low downtime strongly favors GoldenGate
Very large databases favor replication-led cutover
GoldenGate handles upgrade and heterogeneous cutover patterns well
High change rate supports continuous replication choice

negative_weights.when: scope.subset_only == true || scope.need_schema_remap == true || scope.need_reorg == true
negative_weights.reason: Subset or transformation requirements weaken GoldenGate as the primary migration path
Long outage windows reduce the need for GoldenGate complexity

eligibility.when: facts.full_database_move == true && facts.same_platform == true && facts.same_version_family == true
eligibility.reason: Full database physical transport aligns best when platform and version family match
A one-version-family gap may still be workable with careful RMAN transport planning
RMAN transport is intended for full-database moves on the same platform

positive_weights.reason: Large databases favor physical transport over logical unload and reload
A moderate-to-long outage window fits backup and restore style transport
Aligned TDE posture keeps physical encrypted transport viable

negative_weights.reason: Tighter downtime windows reduce the fit for downtime-based physical transport
Large version gaps weaken RMAN transport suitability

eligibility.reason: Cross-platform same-endian moves can use XTTS
XTTS is intended for transportable full-database style moves on compatible endian formats

positive_weights.reason: Large databases often justify transportable tablespace techniques
Cross-platform same-endian alignment is a strong XTTS signal
Aligned TDE posture helps transportable tablespace execution

negative_weights.reason: Large version gaps increase XTTS planning complexity
Tight downtime windows can weaken an XTTS-led offline move
Logical transformation requirements weaken XTTS suitability

eligibility.reason: Target is outside the intended ZDM scope

positive_weights.reason: ZDM is stronger when low-downtime orchestration matters
Supported target strongly favors ZDM orchestration
Full database migrations align well with ZDM orchestration
ZDM planning is valuable when encryption and wallet handling are in scope

negative_weights.reason: Small migrations may not justify ZDM complexity

negative_weights.when: scope.need_schema_remap == true || scope.need_reorg == true || scope.subset_only == true
negative_weights.reason: ZDM is a weaker fit when the move needs logical transformation

companion_tools.name: AUTOUPGRADE
companion_tools.reason: Version upgrade should be attached as a companion workflow
